Off Calendar Jun-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS REBECCA SCHUMACHER and GUIDO PICCININI's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions Against Cross-Defendant Adrian N. Blanco.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

In this multiple-party action defendants/cross-complainants REBECCA SCHUMACHER and GUIDO PICCININI ("MOVING PARTIES") move to compel cross-defendant ADRIAN N. BLANCO ("BLANCO") to provide further responses to their Requests for Production of Documents, Set One ("Requests"). BLANCO opposes only that portion of the motion requesting sanctions.

The Motion primarily seeks a date certain for BLANCO to produce documents, which had been promised, in response to Requests numbered 1 through 10. The Motion also seeks to compel a further response to number 7. BLANCO's response to number 7 included an objection, which was improper given the waiver under Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.300.

Based upon the foregoing, the Motion is GRANTED as follows:

1. BLANCO must serve a further verified response to document request number 7 without objection and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.010, et seq. The further verified response must be served no later than ten (10) days after notice of entry of this Order.

2. BLANCO must produce all documents responsive to Requests numbered 1 through 10 no later than fifteen (15) days after notice of entry of this Order.

3. Sanctions are granted against BLANCO and his counsel of record, jointly and severely, to a lesser extent than requested. Sanctions in the amount of $2,295 are to be paid to counsel for MOVING PARTIES no later than fifteen (15) days after notice of entry of this Order.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at sspaulding@bcsslaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Spaulding; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to all three causes of action, 10 calendar days to answer. "[T]he difficulties in demurring on statute of limitations grounds are clear when the relevant facts are not clear such that the cause of action might be, but is not necessarily time-barred, the demurrer will be overruled." (Austin v. Medicis (2018) 21 Cal. App. 5th 577, 585). The relevant alleged facts on the negligent misrepresentation and professional negligence claims are not clear whether or not the Robins' March 7, 2018 demand put Schumacher and Piccinini on inquiry notice that the work performed by Neumann Sloat and Blanco was not done in accordance with their alleged representations and was performed negligently. The relevant alleged facts on the breach of contract claim are not clear whether or not Schumacher and Piccinini discovered or reasonably should have discovered that they were harmed by the alleged contract breaches prior to Blanco's deposition on January 30, 2020.
